evals.name: encrypt-string-stdout
evals.prompt: Encrypt the string 'database-password-123' using our Alibaba Cloud KMS key alias prod-key. Just print the result to terminal.
evals.expected_output: Three-line base64 output on stdout, correct flags used

evals.name: encrypt-file-to-file
evals.prompt: Encrypt /tmp/secrets.yaml with KMS key alias prod-key and save to /tmp/secrets.yaml.enc

evals.name: encrypt-with-context
evals.prompt: Encrypt 'db://prod/host' with key alias prod-key, and tag it so KMS logs show it's from the billing service in production.

evals.name: encrypt-stdin-pipe
evals.prompt: I have a CI pipeline step that pipes data in. Need to KMS-encrypt whatever comes from stdin using key alias ci-key and pipe the result to stdout.
evals.expected_output: Uses --in-file - for stdin, no --out-file for stdout

evals.name: encrypt-aes128
evals.prompt: Encrypt 'lightweight-secret' with key alias prod-key, but use AES_128 instead of the default 256-bit key.

evals.name: encrypt-number-of-bytes
evals.prompt: I need a custom 16-byte data key for this encryption. Encrypt 'special-key' with key alias prod-key and explicit 16-byte key size.

evals.name: encrypt-missing-input-error
evals.prompt: Run the KMS encrypt command with key alias prod-key but don't specify what to encrypt -- I want to see what error it gives.
evals.expected_output: Command fails with 'either --data or --in-file is required'

evals.name: encrypt-conflict-flags-error
evals.prompt: Try to encrypt using both --data 'test' and --in-file /tmp/x at the same time with key alias prod-key.

evals.name: encrypt-bad-context-error
evals.prompt: Try to encrypt with key alias prod-key and --encryption-context 'not-json' which is invalid.
evals.expected_output: Command fails with JSON parse error
